steps.title: Find the contextual signal
steps.content: The kelp forests slow down powerful and rough currents, and the result is described as having "calmer waters where animals can take shelter." The blank should mean calm or peaceful.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean calm and safe — matching the "calmer waters where animals can take shelter" created by the kelp forests. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — surprising: "a more surprising environment" — surprising means unexpected or astonishing. The passage never suggests the environment is astonishing; it describes a physical outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — dangerous: "a more dangerous environment" — dangerous means harmful or threatening, the opposite of the shelter and calm the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice C — tranquil: "a more tranquil environment" — tranquil means peaceful and calm, which perfectly matches "calmer waters where animals can take shelter." ✓
  • Choice D — imaginative: "a more imaginative environment" — imaginative means creative or inventive. Nothing in the passage describes creativity. ✗
That matches Choice C — tranquil.
